evals.name: init-mode-learn-conventions
evals.prompt: I'd like you to analyze my existing Terraform repo and learn its conventions before we do any CDK-to-Terraform work. Walk the modules under modules/, read the live configs under live/, and summarize the conventions you find. If you have a skill loaded, use its 'init' workflow and persist what you learned to its reference files. Otherwise just produce two markdown files: repo-conventions.md and module-inventory.md.

assertions.text: repo-conventions.md is produced and non-trivial (>800 bytes)
repo-conventions.md has no unfilled <FILL IN> placeholders
repo-conventions.md mentions the module file layout (main.tf/variables.tf/outputs.tf/versions.tf)
repo-conventions.md identifies the snake_case variable convention
repo-conventions.md documents the tagging pattern (common_tags / default_tags / merge)
repo-conventions.md mentions the S3 backend
repo-conventions.md documents the AWS provider version floor (~> 5.70)
repo-conventions.md mentions the eu-central-1 default region
repo-conventions.md identifies the `this` resource-label convention
repo-conventions.md mentions the per-resource SG rule style (aws_vpc_security_group_*_rule)
module-inventory.md is produced and non-trivial (>400 bytes)
module-inventory.md lists the network module
module-inventory.md lists the alb module
module-inventory.md lists the ecs-service module
module-inventory.md lists the iam-role module
module-inventory.md documents Inputs and Outputs for modules

evals.name: convert-greenfield-bedrock-stack
evals.prompt: Convert the CDK .NET 8 project into Terraform that fits the repo. Everything is greenfield. Reuse the modules where they fit (network, alb, ecs-service, iam-role). The stack: ALB → Fargate → Bedrock (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Claude 3 Haiku) with a private VPC endpoint for bedrock-runtime.

assertions.text: New live config directory under live/dev/ with main.tf
New live config has a variables.tf
New live config has a versions.tf
main.tf calls the existing network module
main.tf calls the existing alb module
main.tf calls the existing ecs-service module
Grants bedrock:InvokeModel
Grants bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream
References claude-3-5-sonnet foundation model
References claude-3-haiku foundation model
Creates an interface VPC endpoint for bedrock-runtime
No C# / CDK syntax leaked into .tf files
versions.tf includes a backend "s3" block
Provider region is eu-central-1
No CFN Fn:: / Ref:: tokens remain
Tagging pattern matches repo (default_tags or local.common_tags)

evals.name: convert-with-vpc-import
evals.prompt: Same conversion but the VPC vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8 is already deployed and must be imported via an import block. Everything else is greenfield.

assertions.text: New live config directory under live/dev/ with main.tf
main.tf calls the existing network module (not raw aws_vpc)
main.tf calls the existing alb module
main.tf calls the existing ecs-service module
Grants bedrock:InvokeModel
Grants bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream
Creates an interface VPC endpoint for bedrock-runtime
imports.tf exists in the new live config dir
imports.tf contains an import block with vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8
Import `to =` addresses a VPC resource (module or raw)
No raw aws_vpc resource at the live-config level that would conflict with the module-owned one
No C# / CDK syntax leaked into .tf files
